scan_errors: Group D (tools viral): No single standout viral tool launch this week — broader agentic trend covered instead
Group E (open source): Hugging Face search returned ecosystem overview rather than a specific breakout release
Group K (underreported): Bloomberg/FT/Reuters queries returned mostly paywalled articles; key story (Microsoft clean energy) captured via GeekWire/TechCrunch secondaries

stories.headline: Elon Musk v. Sam Altman trial enters second week as Brockman and Zilis testify about control offers and safety record
stories.summary: The federal trial in Oakland reached its seventh day with OpenAI president Greg Brockman testifying that Musk 'gave up' on the company after learning he would not have control, and Shivon Zilis — a former OpenAI board member and mother of Musk's children — testifying that Musk offered Altman a Tesla board seat. The trial is putting OpenAI's safety commitments under direct scrutiny, with a former employee testifying that product pressure compromised safety work.
stories.raw_excerpt: Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member and mother of Musk's children, testified that Musk offered Altman a Tesla board seat

stories.headline: OpenAI finalizes $10 billion joint venture with 18 private equity firms called The Deployment Company
stories.summary: OpenAI closed a $10 billion joint venture anchored by TPG and 17 other PE firms. The entity, called The Deployment Company, will embed OpenAI engineers directly inside businesses in healthcare, shipping, manufacturing, and finance — a delivery model similar to Palantir's forward-deployed approach. The PE side receives a guaranteed 17.5% annual return for five years.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI has raised more than $4 billion from investors including TPG Inc., Brookfield Asset Management, Advent and Bain Capital

stories.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant as new default ChatGPT model, claims 52% fewer hallucinations
stories.summary: OpenAI replaced GPT-5.3 Instant with GPT-5.5 Instant as the default model for ChatGPT. The company claims internal evaluations show 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts covering medicine, law, and finance. The model also features better personalization drawing on past chats, files, and Gmail.
stories.raw_excerpt: GPT-5.5 Instant produced 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts

stories.headline: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5-Cyber to vetted security teams one week after Anthropic's Mythos vulnerability warning
stories.summary: OpenAI began distributing a cybersecurity-tuned version of its latest model, GPT-5.5-Cyber, to a restricted group of vetted security professionals. The release comes just days after Anthropic's Dario Amodei warned of a 'moment of danger' triggered by Anthropic's own Mythos model finding tens of thousands of software vulnerabilities. Both labs are restricting access to their most security-capable models while simultaneously publishing warnings about the risk of misuse.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI rolled out a variation of its latest AI model in limited preview capacity to vetted cybersecurity teams

stories.headline: OpenAI plans to spend $50 billion on computing in 2026, up from $30 million in 2017
stories.summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman disclosed the company expects to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026 alone. The figure highlights the exponential growth of AI infrastructure costs — from roughly $30 million in 2017 to tens of billions annually — and raises questions about whether this spending level is sustainable for a company still building toward profitability.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI expects to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026 to support its artificial intelligence software

stories.headline: Anthropic CEO warns of cybersecurity 'moment of danger' as Mythos model finds tens of thousands of software vulnerabilities
stories.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly warned that the company's new Mythos model has found tens of thousands of high-severity vulnerabilities across every major operating system and browser — nearly 300 in Firefox alone compared to 20 found by an earlier model. Access to Mythos is restricted to vetted partners under 'Project Glasswing.' Amodei said organizations have a narrow window to patch vulnerabilities before bad actors exploit the same techniques.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned that artificial intelligence has created a narrow window for the world's tech firms, governments and banks to fix tens of thousands of software vulnerabilities

stories.headline: Anthropic signs deal to use all of xAI's Colossus 1 data center — months after Musk called Anthropic a civilization threat
stories.summary: Anthropic announced an agreement with SpaceX — which owns xAI — to use the full compute capacity of Colossus 1, xAI's Memphis data center: 300MW and over 220,000 Nvidia GPUs. Elon Musk had publicly described Anthropic as a company that 'hates Western civilization' in February. The deal's timing — while Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI is in trial — makes Musk's firm a compute supplier to OpenAI's primary rival, despite Musk's stated position that both companies represent existential dangers.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic has signed a deal with SpaceX, which owns rival xAI, to use all of the compute capacity at the company's Colossus 1 data center in Memphis

stories.headline: Anthropic launches $1.5 billion PE venture with Goldman Sachs and Blackstone — announced minutes after OpenAI's parallel JV
stories.summary: Within minutes of OpenAI announcing its $10 billion Deployment Company JV, Anthropic announced a $1.5 billion joint venture with Goldman Sachs, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman to accelerate AI adoption across PE-owned companies. The near-simultaneous announcements raised eyebrows about whether the timing was coordinated, a coincidence, or a competitive reaction. Both ventures target the same sectors: healthcare, finance, and industrial businesses.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic is partnering with private equity giants Goldman Sachs and Blackstone to launch a $1.5 billion firm

stories.headline: Google DeepMind UK workers vote 98% to unionize over Pentagon AI deal that overrides eight years of company ethics pledges
stories.summary: Employees at Google DeepMind's London headquarters voted 98% in favour of forming a union, directly citing Google's decision to let the U.S. Department of Defense use Gemini inside classified military networks 'for any lawful purpose.' Workers demand restoration of Google's previously scrapped weapons pledge, an independent ethics body, and the right to abstain from projects that violate their ethics. Management has ten working days to voluntarily recognise the union before escalation.
stories.raw_excerpt: In a vote of CWU members at DeepMind, 98% backed the bid

stories.headline: US government will pre-test unreleased AI models from Google, Microsoft, and xAI for security vulnerabilities before public deployment
stories.summary: The Commerce Department's Center for Artificial Intelligence Standards and Innovation signed agreements to evaluate pre-release models from Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI for security prior to deployment. The deals are part of a broader shift toward government oversight of frontier AI. Critics note the testing is voluntary, enforcement mechanisms are absent, and the definition of 'security' remains undefined in the published agreement.
stories.raw_excerpt: Google, Microsoft and xAI will share unreleased versions of their AI models with the government to curb cybersecurity threats

stories.headline: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs on May 20 and cancel 6,000 open roles as $135 billion AI spend reshapes workforce into 'pods'
stories.summary: Meta announced it will cut 8,000 employees — roughly 10% of its workforce — on May 20, with further waves planned for H2 2026, while simultaneously raising its AI capital expenditure guidance to $125–145 billion for 2026, up from $72 billion in 2025. Teams are being reorganised into AI-focused 'pods' under a new Chief AI Officer. The framing is explicit: this is reallocation toward AI, not cost reduction.
stories.raw_excerpt: Meta is laying off roughly 8,000 employees on 20 May, 10% of its workforce, with further cuts planned for later in 2026 as it redirects toward AI infrastructure

stories.headline: Meta announces AI system that scans users' height and bone structure to detect underage accounts
stories.summary: Meta announced it will use AI to analyse visual signals including height and bone structure across posts, Reels, and live videos to identify users who may be under 13, then deactivate their accounts pending age verification. The company insists this is not facial recognition. Critics have raised questions about accuracy across demographic groups, the privacy implications of biometric profiling without consent, and whether determined underage users will simply adapt.
stories.raw_excerpt: Meta will use AI to analyze height and bone structure to identify if users are underage

stories.headline: Microsoft weighing abandonment of 2030 carbon-free energy pledge as AI data centers add a gigawatt of capacity every three months
stories.summary: Bloomberg reports Microsoft is in internal discussions about whether to delay or abandon its commitment to match 100% of hourly electricity use with renewable energy by 2030. The company is adding roughly one gigawatt of data center capacity every three months and faces limited renewable supply. Microsoft publicly denies plans to abandon sustainability goals while spending $190 billion on data centers through December — a spending level that implies its climate commitments were made before AI economics were understood.
stories.raw_excerpt: Microsoft is in discussions to shelve one of the industry's most ambitious clean-energy targets, weighing whether to delay or even abandon altogether its 2030 target

stories.headline: Microsoft launches its first voluntary retirement program in 51 years as AI spending tightens non-AI budgets
stories.summary: Microsoft offered roughly 8,750 U.S. employees — about 7% of its domestic workforce — a voluntary buyout with cash severance, continued healthcare, and vested stock, with a June 8 deadline. This is the first such programme in the company's 51-year history. The move comes as Microsoft accelerates AI capital spending while reportedly cutting budgets in divisions not directly tied to AI.
stories.raw_excerpt: Approximately 8,750 Microsoft employees, representing about 7% of its U.S. workforce, face a crucial decision with until June 8 to opt for this voluntary buyout

stories.headline: Federal court pauses Colorado's AI Act enforcement weeks before its effective date after xAI files constitutional challenge
stories.summary: On April 27, a federal court stayed enforcement of Colorado SB 24-205 — one of the most comprehensive state AI laws in the US — blocking investigations until the case is resolved. xAI filed the constitutional challenge; the US Department of Justice intervened to argue the law 'impermissibly compels AI systems to adopt state-defined viewpoints.' Colorado's own governor had previously expressed concern about the law's industry impact, and lawmakers are actively considering a rewrite.
stories.raw_excerpt: A federal court paused enforcement of Colorado's Artificial Intelligence Act, placing one of the country's most comprehensive state AI laws on hold

stories.headline: Researchers expose 15,500-domain AI-themed deepfake investment fraud network using ad trackers to evade detection
stories.summary: Malwarebytes researchers documented a large-scale AI investment fraud operation spanning more than 15,500 domains. The network uses the Keitaro ad-tracking platform as a cloaking layer so real victims see deepfake celebrity endorsements and fake 'AI trading' pitches, while security scanners see harmless pages. Generative AI produces campaign material programmatically at scale across multiple languages. Despite domain takedowns, the operation continues rotating infrastructure with minimal disruption.
stories.raw_excerpt: Researchers tracked a large AI-themed investment scam campaign involving more than 15,000 domains that uses cloaking and deepfakes to hide from security tools

stories.headline: DeepSeek V4 released as open-source — and the lab itself admits it trails US frontier models by three to six months
stories.summary: DeepSeek released a preview of V4, including a 1.6-trillion parameter model under MIT License, built on Huawei's Ascend 950 chips after US export controls blocked Nvidia access. Uniquely, DeepSeek's own documentation acknowledges V4 'trails state-of-the-art frontier models by approximately 3 to 6 months' — a candid admission that contrasts with the outsized market panic triggered by its 2025 R1 release.
stories.raw_excerpt: DeepSeek explicitly acknowledges that V4 trails state-of-the-art frontier models by approximately 3 to 6 months

stories.headline: OpenAI begins testing ads in ChatGPT for free users with Target, Adobe, and Albertsons among first advertisers
stories.summary: OpenAI rolled out ads inside ChatGPT for free and Go-tier users in the US. Participating brands include Target, Adobe, Williams-Sonoma, and Albertsons. As of mid-March, ads had reached about 5% of ChatGPT mobile users. Ads are matched to conversation context, clearly labelled as sponsored, and separated visually from answers. Plus, Pro, and Enterprise tiers will not see ads.
stories.raw_excerpt: Testing ads in ChatGPT — update as of March 26, 2026: early results are encouraging
